Elements in an array can also be **modified**:

```{=html}
<div class="chunk-track" data-chunk-id="w1_c9">
```
```{shinylive-python}
#| standalone: true
#| viewerHeight: 230
#| packages: [numpy]
from shiny import App, ui, render, reactive
import io, contextlib, ast
import numpy as np

CHUNK_ID = "w1_c9"

app_ui = ui.page_fluid(
    ui.tags.script('Shiny.addCustomMessageHandler("chunkRun",function(m){window.parent.postMessage({type:"chunkDone",chunkId:m.chunkId},"*");});'),
    ui.input_text_area("code", "Modifying Array Elements", value="import numpy as np\ny = np.array([9, 3, 7, 2])\ny[0] = 10          # Modify first element (index 0!)\nprint(y)\n\ny[:] = 8           # Modify all elements\nprint(y)\n\ny = 8              # Reassign variable y\nprint(y)", width='100%', height='165px'),
    ui.input_action_button("run", "Run Code", class_="btn-success"),
    ui.output_text_verbatim("out")
)
def server(input, output, session):
    @render.text
    @reactive.event(input.run)
    async def out():
        await session.send_custom_message("chunkRun", {"chunkId": CHUNK_ID})
        f = io.StringIO(); ns = {}
        with contextlib.redirect_stdout(f):
            try:
                tree = ast.parse(input.code())
                for node in tree.body:
                    if isinstance(node, ast.Expr):
                        val = eval(compile(ast.Expression(node.value),'<s>','eval'), ns)
                        if val is not None: print(val)
                    else: exec(compile(ast.Module([node],[]),'<s>','exec'), ns)
            except Exception as e: print(f"Error: {e}")
        return f.getvalue()
app = App(app_ui, server)
```
```{=html}
</div>
```

---

## 3 – **Datatype:** Matrices

A matrix is a two-dimensional array of numbers. In Python we use **NumPy** with `reshape()`:

```{=html}
<div class="chunk-track" data-chunk-id="w1_c10">
```
```{shinylive-python}
#| standalone: true
#| viewerHeight: 230
#| packages: [numpy]
from shiny import App, ui, render, reactive
import io, contextlib, ast
import numpy as np

CHUNK_ID = "w1_c10"

app_ui = ui.page_fluid(
    ui.tags.script('Shiny.addCustomMessageHandler("chunkRun",function(m){window.parent.postMessage({type:"chunkDone",chunkId:m.chunkId},"*");});'),
    ui.input_text_area("code", "Creating Matrices", value="import numpy as np\nx = np.array([1,2,3,4,5,6])\n\n# Fill row-wise (like byrow=TRUE in R)\nX = x.reshape(2, 3)\nprint(X)\n\n# Fill column-wise (like byrow=FALSE in R)\nX = x.reshape(2, 3, order='F')\nprint(X)", width='100%', height='165px'),
    ui.input_action_button("run", "Run Code", class_="btn-success"),
    ui.output_text_verbatim("out")
)
def server(input, output, session):
    @render.text
    @reactive.event(input.run)
    async def out():
        await session.send_custom_message("chunkRun", {"chunkId": CHUNK_ID})
        f = io.StringIO(); ns = {}
        with contextlib.redirect_stdout(f):
            try:
                tree = ast.parse(input.code())
                for node in tree.body:
                    if isinstance(node, ast.Expr):
                        val = eval(compile(ast.Expression(node.value),'<s>','eval'), ns)
                        if val is not None: print(val)
                    else: exec(compile(ast.Module([node],[]),'<s>','exec'), ns)
            except Exception as e: print(f"Error: {e}")
        return f.getvalue()
app = App(app_ui, server)
```
```{=html}
</div>
```

With `.shape` you can query the **dimensions** of a matrix:

```{=html}
<div class="chunk-track" data-chunk-id="w1_c11">
```
```{shinylive-python}
#| standalone: true
#| viewerHeight: 230
#| packages: [numpy]
from shiny import App, ui, render, reactive
import io, contextlib, ast
import numpy as np

CHUNK_ID = "w1_c11"

app_ui = ui.page_fluid(
    ui.tags.script('Shiny.addCustomMessageHandler("chunkRun",function(m){window.parent.postMessage({type:"chunkDone",chunkId:m.chunkId},"*");});'),
    ui.input_text_area("code", "shape", value="import numpy as np\nX = np.arange(1, 7).reshape(2, 3)\nprint(X.shape)   # (2, 3) → 2 rows, 3 columns", width='100%', height='80px'),
    ui.input_action_button("run", "Run Code", class_="btn-success"),
    ui.output_text_verbatim("out")
)
def server(input, output, session):
    @render.text
    @reactive.event(input.run)
    async def out():
        await session.send_custom_message("chunkRun", {"chunkId": CHUNK_ID})
        f = io.StringIO(); ns = {}
        with contextlib.redirect_stdout(f):
            try:
                tree = ast.parse(input.code())
                for node in tree.body:
                    if isinstance(node, ast.Expr):
                        val = eval(compile(ast.Expression(node.value),'<s>','eval'), ns)
                        if val is not None: print(val)
                    else: exec(compile(ast.Module([node],[]),'<s>','exec'), ns)
            except Exception as e: print(f"Error: {e}")
        return f.getvalue()
app = App(app_ui, server)
```
```{=html}
</div>
```

Elements of a matrix are accessed with **two indices** `[row, column]` – indexing also starts at 0 here:

```{=html}
<div class="chunk-track" data-chunk-id="w1_c12">
```
```{shinylive-python}
#| standalone: true
#| viewerHeight: 230
#| packages: [numpy]
from shiny import App, ui, render, reactive
import io, contextlib, ast
import numpy as np

CHUNK_ID = "w1_c12"

app_ui = ui.page_fluid(
    ui.tags.script('Shiny.addCustomMessageHandler("chunkRun",function(m){window.parent.postMessage({type:"chunkDone",chunkId:m.chunkId},"*");});'),
    ui.input_text_area("code", "Matrix Access", value="import numpy as np\nX = np.arange(1, 7).reshape(2, 3)\nprint(X[0, 1])       # Element in row 0, column 1\nprint(X[0:2, 0])     # Rows 0-1, column 0\nprint(X[1, :])       # All elements of the 2nd row\nprint(len(X[0:2, 0]))", width='100%', height='155px'),
    ui.input_action_button("run", "Run Code", class_="btn-success"),
    ui.output_text_verbatim("out")
)
def server(input, output, session):
    @render.text
    @reactive.event(input.run)
    async def out():
        await session.send_custom_message("chunkRun", {"chunkId": CHUNK_ID})
        f = io.StringIO(); ns = {}
        with contextlib.redirect_stdout(f):
            try:
                tree = ast.parse(input.code())
                for node in tree.body:
                    if isinstance(node, ast.Expr):
                        val = eval(compile(ast.Expression(node.value),'<s>','eval'), ns)
                        if val is not None: print(val)
                    else: exec(compile(ast.Module([node],[]),'<s>','exec'), ns)
            except Exception as e: print(f"Error: {e}")
        return f.getvalue()
app = App(app_ui, server)
```
```{=html}
</div>
```

With `np.column_stack()` column vectors can be joined into a matrix. Column names can be assigned via a pandas DataFrame:

```{=html}
<div class="chunk-track" data-chunk-id="w1_c13">
```
```{shinylive-python}
#| standalone: true
#| viewerHeight: 230
#| packages: [numpy]
from shiny import App, ui, render, reactive
import io, contextlib, ast
import numpy as np

CHUNK_ID = "w1_c13"

app_ui = ui.page_fluid(
    ui.tags.script('Shiny.addCustomMessageHandler("chunkRun",function(m){window.parent.postMessage({type:"chunkDone",chunkId:m.chunkId},"*");});'),
    ui.input_text_area("code", "column_stack() and Names", value="import numpy as np\nimport pandas as pd\n\nx = np.arange(1, 6)\ny = np.arange(6, 11)\nz = np.arange(11, 16)\nA = np.column_stack([x, y, z])\n\n# With row and column names as DataFrame:\ndf = pd.DataFrame(A, index=list('abcde'), columns=['patient1','patient2','patient3'])\nprint(df)", width='100%', height='185px'),
    ui.input_action_button("run", "Run Code", class_="btn-success"),
    ui.output_text_verbatim("out")
)
def server(input, output, session):
    @render.text
    @reactive.event(input.run)
    async def out():
        await session.send_custom_message("chunkRun", {"chunkId": CHUNK_ID})
        f = io.StringIO(); ns = {}
        with contextlib.redirect_stdout(f):
            try:
                tree = ast.parse(input.code())
                for node in tree.body:
                    if isinstance(node, ast.Expr):
                        val = eval(compile(ast.Expression(node.value),'<s>','eval'), ns)
                        if val is not None: print(val)
                    else: exec(compile(ast.Module([node],[]),'<s>','exec'), ns)
            except Exception as e: print(f"Error: {e}")
        return f.getvalue()
app = App(app_ui, server)
```
```{=html}
</div>
```

> 💡 If you need more information on a function, type `help(np.reshape)` in the sandbox or visit the [NumPy documentation](https://numpy.org/doc/).

---

## 4 – **Datatype:** Dictionaries (Lists)

In Python, **dictionaries** (`dict`) correspond to named lists in R. They can contain **various types of elements**:

```{=html}
<div class="chunk-track" data-chunk-id="w1_c14">
```
```{shinylive-python}
#| standalone: true
#| viewerHeight: 240
from shiny import App, ui, render, reactive
import io, contextlib, ast

CHUNK_ID = "w1_c14"

app_ui = ui.page_fluid(
    ui.tags.script('Shiny.addCustomMessageHandler("chunkRun",function(m){window.parent.postMessage({type:"chunkDone",chunkId:m.chunkId},"*");});'),
    ui.input_text_area("code", "Creating a Dictionary", value="dict1 = {'color': ['blue', 'red'], 'size': 5, 'state': True}\nprint(dict1)", width='100%', height='100px'),
    ui.input_action_button("run", "Run Code", class_="btn-success"),
    ui.output_text_verbatim("out")
)
def server(input, output, session):
    @render.text
    @reactive.event(input.run)
    async def out():
        await session.send_custom_message("chunkRun", {"chunkId": CHUNK_ID})
        f = io.StringIO(); ns = {}
        with contextlib.redirect_stdout(f):
            try:
                tree = ast.parse(input.code())
                for node in tree.body:
                    if isinstance(node, ast.Expr):
                        val = eval(compile(ast.Expression(node.value),'<s>','eval'), ns)
                        if val is not None: print(val)
                    else: exec(compile(ast.Module([node],[]),'<s>','exec'), ns)
            except Exception as e: print(f"Error: {e}")
        return f.getvalue()
app = App(app_ui, server)
```
```{=html}
</div>
```

Dictionary elements can be accessed with `[]` or `.get()`. A single key returns the value directly – note the **difference** to R lists:

```{=html}
<div class="chunk-track" data-chunk-id="w1_c15">
```
```{shinylive-python}
#| standalone: true
#| viewerHeight: 280
from shiny import App, ui, render, reactive
import io, contextlib, ast

CHUNK_ID = "w1_c15"

app_ui = ui.page_fluid(
    ui.tags.script('Shiny.addCustomMessageHandler("chunkRun",function(m){window.parent.postMessage({type:"chunkDone",chunkId:m.chunkId},"*");});'),
    ui.input_text_area("code", "Dictionary Access", value="dict1 = {'color': ['blue', 'red'], 'size': 5, 'state': True}\nprint(dict1['color'])            # Content of 'color'\nprint(len(dict1['color']))       # Length: 2\n\n# A single key returns the value directly:\nprint(type(dict1['color']))      # list\n# dict1['color'][1] – second element\nprint(dict1['color'][1])", width='100%', height='165px'),
    ui.input_action_button("run", "Run Code", class_="btn-success"),
    ui.output_text_verbatim("out")
)
def server(input, output, session):
    @render.text
    @reactive.event(input.run)
    async def out():
        await session.send_custom_message("chunkRun", {"chunkId": CHUNK_ID})
        f = io.StringIO(); ns = {}
        with contextlib.redirect_stdout(f):
            try:
                tree = ast.parse(input.code())
                for node in tree.body:
                    if isinstance(node, ast.Expr):
                        val = eval(compile(ast.Expression(node.value),'<s>','eval'), ns)
                        if val is not None: print(val)
                    else: exec(compile(ast.Module([node],[]),'<s>','exc'), ns)
            except Exception as e: print(f"Error: {e}")
        return f.getvalue()
app = App(app_ui, server)
```
```{=html}
</div>
```

For **unnamed** structures, a plain `list` is used in Python:

```{=html}
<div class="chunk-track" data-chunk-id="w1_c16">
```
```{shinylive-python}
#| standalone: true
#| viewerHeight: 260
from shiny import App, ui, render, reactive
import io, contextlib, ast

CHUNK_ID = "w1_c16"

app_ui = ui.page_fluid(
    ui.tags.script('Shiny.addCustomMessageHandler("chunkRun",function(m){window.parent.postMessage({type:"chunkDone",chunkId:m.chunkId},"*");});'),
    ui.input_text_area("code", "Unnamed Lists", value="list2 = [['blue', 'red'], 5, True]\nprint(list2[0])      # First element\nprint(list2[0][1])   # Second position of the first element", width='100%', height='110px'),
    ui.input_action_button("run", "Run Code", class_="btn-success"),
    ui.output_text_verbatim("out")
)
def server(input, output, session):
    @render.text
    @reactive.event(input.run)
    async def out():
        await session.send_custom_message("chunkRun", {"chunkId": CHUNK_ID})
        f = io.StringIO(); ns = {}
        with contextlib.redirect_stdout(f):
            try:
                tree = ast.parse(input.code())
                for node in tree.body:
                    if isinstance(node, ast.Expr):
                        val = eval(compile(ast.Expression(node.value),'<s>','eval'), ns)
                        if val is not None: print(val)
                    else: exec(compile(ast.Module([node],[]),'<s>','exec'), ns)
            except Exception as e: print(f"Error: {e}")
        return f.getvalue()
app = App(app_ui, server)
```
```{=html}
</div>
```

Deleting an element from a dictionary or list:

```{=html}
<div class="chunk-track" data-chunk-id="w1_c17">
```
```{shinylive-python}
#| standalone: true
#| viewerHeight: 220
from shiny import App, ui, render, reactive
import io, contextlib, ast

CHUNK_ID = "w1_c17"

app_ui = ui.page_fluid(
    ui.tags.script('Shiny.addCustomMessageHandler("chunkRun",function(m){window.parent.postMessage({type:"chunkDone",chunkId:m.chunkId},"*");});'),
    ui.input_text_area("code", "Removing an Element", value="list2 = [['blue', 'red'], 5, True]\ndel list2[2]   # Delete the third element\nprint(list2)", width='100%', height='100px'),
    ui.input_action_button("run", "Run Code", class_="btn-success"),
    ui.output_text_verbatim("out")
)
def server(input, output, session):
    @render.text
    @reactive.event(input.run)
    async def out():
        await session.send_custom_message("chunkRun", {"chunkId": CHUNK_ID})
        f = io.StringIO(); ns = {}
        with contextlib.redirect_stdout(f):
            try:
                tree = ast.parse(input.code())
                for node in tree.body:
                    if isinstance(node, ast.Expr):
                        val = eval(compile(ast.Expression(node.value),'<s>','eval'), ns)
                        if val is not None: print(val)
                    else: exec(compile(ast.Module([node],[]),'<s>','exec'), ns)
            except Exception as e: print(f"Error: {e}")
        return f.getvalue()
app = App(app_ui, server)
```
```{=html}
</div>
```

---

## 5 – **Datatype:** DataFrames

DataFrames are like matrices, but can contain **various data types** in different columns. In Python we use **pandas**:

```{=html}
<div class="chunk-track" data-chunk-id="w1_c18">
```
```{shinylive-python}
#| standalone: true
#| viewerHeight: 270
from shiny import App, ui, render, reactive
import io, contextlib, ast

CHUNK_ID = "w1_c18"

app_ui = ui.page_fluid(
    ui.tags.script('Shiny.addCustomMessageHandler("chunkRun",function(m){window.parent.postMessage({type:"chunkDone",chunkId:m.chunkId},"*");});'),
    ui.input_text_area("code", "Creating a DataFrame", value="import pandas as pd\n\nName = ['Ben', 'Alice', 'Daniel', 'Charlie']\nYear = [1990, 1992, 1995, 1993]\nOption = ['Biology','Physics', 'Chemistry', 'Mathematics']\n\nStudents = pd.DataFrame({'First_Name': Name, 'Year': Year, 'Options': Option})\nprint(Students)", width='100%', height='165px'),
    ui.input_action_button("run", "Run Code", class_="btn-success"),
    ui.output_text_verbatim("out")
)
def server(input, output, session):
    @render.text
    @reactive.event(input.run)
    async def out():
        await session.send_custom_message("chunkRun", {"chunkId": CHUNK_ID})
        f = io.StringIO(); ns = {}
        with contextlib.redirect_stdout(f):
            try:
                tree = ast.parse(input.code())
                for node in tree.body:
                    if isinstance(node, ast.Expr):
                        val = eval(compile(ast.Expression(node.value),'<s>','eval'), ns)
                        if val is not None: print(val)
                    else: exec(compile(ast.Module([node],[]),'<s>','exec'), ns)
            except Exception as e: print(f"Error: {e}")
        return f.getvalue()
app = App(app_ui, server)
```
```{=html}
</div>
```

DataFrames can be indexed similarly to dictionaries and matrices:

```{=html}
<div class="chunk-track" data-chunk-id="w1_c19">
```
```{shinylive-python}
#| standalone: true
#| viewerHeight: 300
from shiny import App, ui, render, reactive
import io, contextlib, ast

CHUNK_ID = "w1_c19"

app_ui = ui.page_fluid(
    ui.tags.script('Shiny.addCustomMessageHandler("chunkRun",function(m){window.parent.postMessage({type:"chunkDone",chunkId:m.chunkId},"*");});'),
    ui.input_text_area("code", "DataFrame Access", value="import pandas as pd\nName = ['Ben', 'Alice', 'Daniel', 'Charlie']\nYear = [1990, 1992, 1995, 1993]\nOption = ['Biology','Physics', 'Chemistry', 'Mathematics']\nStudents = pd.DataFrame({'First_Name': Name, 'Year': Year, 'Options': Option})\n\nprint(Students['Year'])            # Column 'Year'\nprint(Students.iloc[:, 1])         # Column by index\nprint(Students['Options'].iloc[2]) # 3rd element (index 2!)\nprint(Students.iloc[1:3, 2])       # Rows 1-2, column 2", width='100%', height='195px'),
    ui.input_action_button("run", "Run Code", class_="btn-success"),
    ui.output_text_verbatim("out")
)
def server(input, output, session):
    @render.text
    @reactive.event(input.run)
    async def out():
        await session.send_custom_message("chunkRun", {"chunkId": CHUNK_ID})
        f = io.StringIO(); ns = {}
        with contextlib.redirect_stdout(f):
            try:
                tree = ast.parse(input.code())
                for node in tree.body:
                    if isinstance(node, ast.Expr):
                        val = eval(compile(ast.Expression(node.value),'<s>','eval'), ns)
                        if val is not None: print(val)
                    else: exec(compile(ast.Module([node],[]),'<s>','exec'), ns)
            except Exception as e: print(f"Error: {e}")
        return f.getvalue()
app = App(app_ui, server)
```
```{=html}
</div>
```

There are important **differences** between DataFrames and NumPy matrices. Note especially: `Df[3]` returns the column with label 3, not the 4th element!

```{=html}
<div class="chunk-track" data-chunk-id="w1_c20">
```
```{shinylive-python}
#| standalone: true
#| viewerHeight: 230
#| packages: [numpy]
from shiny import App, ui, render, reactive
import io, contextlib, ast
import numpy as np

CHUNK_ID = "w1_c20"

app_ui = ui.page_fluid(
    ui.tags.script('Shiny.addCustomMessageHandler("chunkRun",function(m){window.parent.postMessage({type:"chunkDone",chunkId:m.chunkId},"*");});'),
    ui.input_text_area("code", "DataFrame vs. Matrix + merge()", value="import numpy as np, pandas as pd\nMat = np.arange(1, 21).reshape(4, 5)\nDf = pd.DataFrame(Mat)\n\nprint(Mat[1,2])      # Single element\nprint(Df.iloc[1,2])  # Single element\n\nprint(Mat[3])        # 4th row (numpy: row!)\nprint(Df[3])         # Column with label 3 (pandas!)\n\n# merge() example:\nName=['Ben','Alice','Daniel','Charlie']; Year=[1990,1992,1995,1993]\nOption=['Biology','Physics','Chemistry','Mathematics']\nStudents=pd.DataFrame({'First_Name':Name,'Year':Year,'Options':Option})\nFull_Names=pd.DataFrame({'First_Name':['Alice','Ben','Daniel'],'Last_Name':['Parker','James','Brown']})\nprint(pd.merge(Students,Full_Names))              # inner join only\nprint(pd.merge(Students,Full_Names,how='outer'))  # all entries", width='100%', height='240px'),
    ui.input_action_button("run", "Run Code", class_="btn-success"),
    ui.output_text_verbatim("out")
)
def server(input, output, session):
    @render.text
    @reactive.event(input.run)
    async def out():
        await session.send_custom_message("chunkRun", {"chunkId": CHUNK_ID})
        f = io.StringIO(); ns = {}
        with contextlib.redirect_stdout(f):
            try:
                tree = ast.parse(input.code())
                for node in tree.body:
                    if isinstance(node, ast.Expr):
                        val = eval(compile(ast.Expression(node.value),'<s>','eval'), ns)
                        if val is not None: print(val)
                    else: exec(compile(ast.Module([node],[]),'<s>','exec'), ns)
            except Exception as e: print(f"Error: {e}")
        return f.getvalue()
app = App(app_ui, server)
```
```{=html}
</div>
```

---

## 6 – **Data Manipulation:** Loops

In Python entspricht `apply()` dem Aufruf von Funktionen mit List Comprehensions oder `numpy`-Operationen. Für Listen und Vektoren nutzt man `map()` oder List Comprehensions.

### map() and list comprehensions

```{=html}
<div class="chunk-track" data-chunk-id="w1_c21">
```
```{shinylive-python}
#| standalone: true
#| viewerHeight: 300
#| packages: [numpy]
from shiny import App, ui, render, reactive
import io, contextlib, ast
import numpy as np

CHUNK_ID = "w1_c21"

app_ui = ui.page_fluid(
    ui.tags.script('Shiny.addCustomMessageHandler("chunkRun",function(m){window.parent.postMessage({type:"chunkDone",chunkId:m.chunkId},"*");});'),
    ui.input_text_area("code", "apply() equivalent – numpy", value="import numpy as np\nMat = np.arange(1, 13).reshape(3, 4)\nprint(Mat)\n\n# Minimum of each row (axis=1):\nprint(np.min(Mat, axis=1))\n\n# Minimum of each column (axis=0):\nprint(np.min(Mat, axis=0))", width='100%', height='160px'),
    ui.input_action_button("run", "Run Code", class_="btn-success"),
    ui.output_text_verbatim("out")
)
def server(input, output, session):
    @render.text
    @reactive.event(input.run)
    async def out():
        await session.send_custom_message("chunkRun", {"chunkId": CHUNK_ID})
        f = io.StringIO(); ns = {}
        with contextlib.redirect_stdout(f):
            try:
                tree = ast.parse(input.code())
                for node in tree.body:
                    if isinstance(node, ast.Expr):
                        val = eval(compile(ast.Expression(node.value),'<s>','eval'), ns)
                        if val is not None: print(val)
                    else: exec(compile(ast.Module([node],[]),'<s>','exec'), ns)
            except Exception as e: print(f"Error: {e}")
        return f.getvalue()
app = App(app_ui, server)
```
```{=html}
</div>
```

`map()` applies a function to each element of an iterable and returns an iterator. `list()` converts it to a list:

```{=html}
<div class="chunk-track" data-chunk-id="w1_c22">
```
```{shinylive-python}
#| standalone: true
#| viewerHeight: 260
from shiny import App, ui, render, reactive
import io, contextlib, ast

CHUNK_ID = "w1_c22"

app_ui = ui.page_fluid(
    ui.tags.script('Shiny.addCustomMessageHandler("chunkRun",function(m){window.parent.postMessage({type:"chunkDone",chunkId:m.chunkId},"*");});'),
    ui.input_text_area("code", "map() and list comprehension", value="import math\nx = [1, 2, 3, 4, 5]\n\n# map() – like sapply():\nprint(list(map(math.sqrt, x)))\n\n# List comprehension – more Pythonic:\nprint([i * 2 for i in x])", width='100%', height='130px'),
    ui.input_action_button("run", "Run Code", class_="btn-success"),
    ui.output_text_verbatim("out")
)
def server(input, output, session):
    @render.text
    @reactive.event(input.run)
    async def out():
        await session.send_custom_message("chunkRun", {"chunkId": CHUNK_ID})
        f = io.StringIO(); ns = {}
        with contextlib.redirect_stdout(f):
            try:
                tree = ast.parse(input.code())
                for node in tree.body:
                    if isinstance(node, ast.Expr):
                        val = eval(compile(ast.Expression(node.value),'<s>','eval'), ns)
                        if val is not None: print(val)
                    else: exec(compile(ast.Module([node],[]),'<s>','exec'), ns)
            except Exception as e: print(f"Error: {e}")
        return f.getvalue()
app = App(app_ui, server)
```
```{=html}
</div>
```

For **lists** (like R's `lapply`/`sapply`), use list comprehensions or `map()`:

```{=html}
<div class="chunk-track" data-chunk-id="w1_c23">
```
```{shinylive-python}
#| standalone: true
#| viewerHeight: 260
from shiny import App, ui, render, reactive
import io, contextlib, ast

CHUNK_ID = "w1_c23"

app_ui = ui.page_fluid(
    ui.tags.script('Shiny.addCustomMessageHandler("chunkRun",function(m){window.parent.postMessage({type:"chunkDone",chunkId:m.chunkId},"*");});'),
    ui.input_text_area("code", "map() on lists (like lapply/sapply)", value="dict1 = {'color': ['blue','red'], 'size': [5], 'state': [True, False, True, True]}\n\n# Length of each value – like sapply(List1, length):\nresult = {k: len(v) for k, v in dict1.items()}\nprint(result)", width='100%', height='120px'),
    ui.input_action_button("run", "Run Code", class_="btn-success"),
    ui.output_text_verbatim("out")
)
def server(input, output, session):
    @render.text
    @reactive.event(input.run)
    async def out():
        await session.send_custom_message("chunkRun", {"chunkId": CHUNK_ID})
        f = io.StringIO(); ns = {}
        with contextlib.redirect_stdout(f):
            try:
                tree = ast.parse(input.code())
                for node in tree.body:
                    if isinstance(node, ast.Expr):
                        val = eval(compile(ast.Expression(node.value),'<s>','eval'), ns)
                        if val is not None: print(val)
                    else: exec(compile(ast.Module([node],[]),'<s>','exec'), ns)
            except Exception as e: print(f"Error: {e}")
        return f.getvalue()
app = App(app_ui, server)
```
```{=html}
</div>
```

### for loops

```{=html}
<div class="chunk-track" data-chunk-id="w1_c24">
```
```{shinylive-python}
#| standalone: true
#| viewerHeight: 260
from shiny import App, ui, render, reactive
import io, contextlib, ast

CHUNK_ID = "w1_c24"

app_ui = ui.page_fluid(
    ui.tags.script('Shiny.addCustomMessageHandler("chunkRun",function(m){window.parent.postMessage({type:"chunkDone",chunkId:m.chunkId},"*");});'),
    ui.input_text_area("code", "for loops", value="x = ['a', 'b', 'c']\nfor i in x:\n    print(i)\n\nfor i in range(1, 5):\n    print(i * 3)", width='100%', height='130px'),
    ui.input_action_button("run", "Run Code", class_="btn-success"),
    ui.output_text_verbatim("out")
)
def server(input, output, session):
    @render.text
    @reactive.event(input.run)
    async def out():
        await session.send_custom_message("chunkRun", {"chunkId": CHUNK_ID})
        f = io.StringIO(); ns = {}
        with contextlib.redirect_stdout(f):
            try:
                tree = ast.parse(input.code())
                for node in tree.body:
                    if isinstance(node, ast.Expr):
                        val = eval(compile(ast.Expression(node.value),'<s>','eval'), ns)
                        if val is not None: print(val)
                    else: exec(compile(ast.Module([node],[]),'<s>','exec'), ns)
            except Exception as e: print(f"Error: {e}")
        return f.getvalue()
app = App(app_ui, server)
```
```{=html}
</div>
```

---

## 7 – **Data Manipulation:** If Statements

```{=html}
<div class="chunk-track" data-chunk-id="w1_c25">
```
```{shinylive-python}
#| standalone: true
#| viewerHeight: 280
from shiny import App, ui, render, reactive
import io, contextlib, ast

CHUNK_ID = "w1_c25"

app_ui = ui.page_fluid(
    ui.tags.script('Shiny.addCustomMessageHandler("chunkRun",function(m){window.parent.postMessage({type:"chunkDone",chunkId:m.chunkId},"*");});'),
    ui.input_text_area("code", "if / elif / else", value="a = 10\nb = 5\n\nif a < b:\n    print('a is smaller than b')\nelif a == b:\n    print('a is equal to b')\nelse:\n    print('a is greater than b')", width='100%', height='160px'),
    ui.input_action_button("run", "Run Code", class_="btn-success"),
    ui.output_text_verbatim("out")
)
def server(input, output, session):
    @render.text
    @reactive.event(input.run)
    async def out():
        await session.send_custom_message("chunkRun", {"chunkId": CHUNK_ID})
        f = io.StringIO(); ns = {}
        with contextlib.redirect_stdout(f):
            try:
                tree = ast.parse(input.code())
                for node in tree.body:
                    if isinstance(node, ast.Expr):
                        val = eval(compile(ast.Expression(node.value),'<s>','eval'), ns)
                        if val is not None: print(val)
                    else: exec(compile(ast.Module([node],[]),'<s>','exec'), ns)
            except Exception as e: print(f"Error: {e}")
        return f.getvalue()
app = App(app_ui, server)
```
```{=html}
</div>
```

The compact Python equivalent of `ifelse()` is the **ternary expression**:

```{=html}
<div class="chunk-track" data-chunk-id="w1_c26">
```
```{shinylive-python}
#| standalone: true
#| viewerHeight: 220
from shiny import App, ui, render, reactive
import io, contextlib, ast

CHUNK_ID = "w1_c26"

app_ui = ui.page_fluid(
    ui.tags.script('Shiny.addCustomMessageHandler("chunkRun",function(m){window.parent.postMessage({type:"chunkDone",chunkId:m.chunkId},"*");});'),
    ui.input_text_area("code", "Ternary expression (like ifelse)", value="a = 5\nb = 10\nresult = 'a is smaller than b' if a < b else 'a is greater or equal to b'\nprint(result)", width='100%', height='90px'),
    ui.input_action_button("run", "Run Code", class_="btn-success"),
    ui.output_text_verbatim("out")
)
def server(input, output, session):
    @render.text
    @reactive.event(input.run)
    async def out():
        await session.send_custom_message("chunkRun", {"chunkId": CHUNK_ID})
        f = io.StringIO(); ns = {}
        with contextlib.redirect_stdout(f):
            try:
                tree = ast.parse(input.code())
                for node in tree.body:
                    if isinstance(node, ast.Expr):
                        val = eval(compile(ast.Expression(node.value),'<s>','eval'), ns)
                        if val is not None: print(val)
                    else: exec(compile(ast.Module([node],[]),'<s>','exec'), ns)
            except Exception as e: print(f"Error: {e}")
        return f.getvalue()
app = App(app_ui, server)
```
```{=html}
</div>
```

---

## 8 – **Data Manipulation:** Subset Selection

### np.where() (equivalent to which())

```{=html}
<div class="chunk-track" data-chunk-id="w1_c27">
```
```{shinylive-python}
#| standalone: true
#| viewerHeight: 260
#| packages: [numpy]
from shiny import App, ui, render, reactive
import io, contextlib, ast
import numpy as np

CHUNK_ID = "w1_c27"

app_ui = ui.page_fluid(
    ui.tags.script('Shiny.addCustomMessageHandler("chunkRun",function(m){window.parent.postMessage({type:"chunkDone",chunkId:m.chunkId},"*");});'),
    ui.input_text_area("code", "np.where() – like which()", value="import numpy as np\na = np.array([3, 5, 10, 4, 2, 6, 7, 2])\n\nnp.where(a < 5)        # positions of elements < 5\na[np.where(a < 5)]     # values of elements < 5", width='100%', height='110px'),
    ui.input_action_button("run", "Run Code", class_="btn-success"),
    ui.output_text_verbatim("out")
)
def server(input, output, session):
    @render.text
    @reactive.event(input.run)
    async def out():
        await session.send_custom_message("chunkRun", {"chunkId": CHUNK_ID})
        f = io.StringIO(); ns = {}
        with contextlib.redirect_stdout(f):
            try:
                tree = ast.parse(input.code())
                for node in tree.body:
                    if isinstance(node, ast.Expr):
                        val = eval(compile(ast.Expression(node.value),'<s>','eval'), ns)
                        if val is not None: print(val)
                    else: exec(compile(ast.Module([node],[]),'<s>','exec'), ns)
            except Exception as e: print(f"Error: {e}")
        return f.getvalue()
app = App(app_ui, server)
```
```{=html}
</div>
```

### np.isin() (equivalent to %in%)

```{=html}
<div class="chunk-track" data-chunk-id="w1_c28">
```
```{shinylive-python}
#| standalone: true
#| viewerHeight: 270
#| packages: [numpy]
from shiny import App, ui, render, reactive
import io, contextlib, ast
import numpy as np

CHUNK_ID = "w1_c28"

app_ui = ui.page_fluid(
    ui.tags.script('Shiny.addCustomMessageHandler("chunkRun",function(m){window.parent.postMessage({type:"chunkDone",chunkId:m.chunkId},"*");});'),
    ui.input_text_area("code", "np.isin() – like %in%", value="import numpy as np\nx = np.array(['a', 'h', 'f', 'b', 'd'])\ny = np.array(['a', 'b', 'c', 'd'])\n\nnp.isin(x, y)                    # logical array\nnp.where(np.isin(x, y))          # positions\nx[np.where(np.isin(x, y))]       # values", width='100%', height='130px'),
    ui.input_action_button("run", "Run Code", class_="btn-success"),
    ui.output_text_verbatim("out")
)
def server(input, output, session):
    @render.text
    @reactive.event(input.run)
    async def out():
        await session.send_custom_message("chunkRun", {"chunkId": CHUNK_ID})
        f = io.StringIO(); ns = {}
        with contextlib.redirect_stdout(f):
            try:
                tree = ast.parse(input.code())
                for node in tree.body:
                    if isinstance(node, ast.Expr):
                        val = eval(compile(ast.Expression(node.value),'<s>','eval'), ns)
                        if val is not None: print(val)
                    else: exec(compile(ast.Module([node],[]),'<s>','exec'), ns)
            except Exception as e: print(f"Error: {e}")
        return f.getvalue()
app = App(app_ui, server)
```
```{=html}
</div>
```

---

## EXERCISES - Datatypes

Answer the questions correctly to save your progress. You can use the code sandboxes to experiment!

---

### **Exercise 1:** Lists & Arrays

```{=html}
<div class="quiz-card">
  <h5>QUIZ: LISTS &amp; ARRAYS</h5>
  <p class="text-muted">🧪 Try out your code here:</p>
```

```{shinylive-python}
#| standalone: true
#| viewerHeight: 230
#| packages: [numpy]
from shiny import App, ui, render, reactive
import io, contextlib, ast
import numpy as np

app_ui = ui.page_fluid(
    ui.input_text_area("code", "Sandbox – Lists & Arrays", value="import numpy as np\n# Try anything here!\n", width='100%', height='100px'),
    ui.input_action_button("run", "Run Code", class_="btn-success"),
    ui.output_text_verbatim("out")
)
def server(input, output, session):
    @render.text
    @reactive.event(input.run)
    async def out():
        f = io.StringIO(); ns = {}
        with contextlib.redirect_stdout(f):
            try:
                tree = ast.parse(input.code())
                for node in tree.body:
                    if isinstance(node, ast.Expr):
                        val = eval(compile(ast.Expression(node.value),'<s>','eval'), ns)
                        if val is not None: print(val)
                    else: exec(compile(ast.Module([node],[]),'<s>','exec'), ns)
            except Exception as e: print(f"Error: {e}")
        return f.getvalue()
app = App(app_ui, server)
```

```{=html}
  <div class="mb-4 mt-3">
    <p><strong>Question 1:</strong> What does <code>len([9, 3, 7, 2])</code> return?</p>
    <div class="form-check"><input class="form-check-input" type="radio" name="pv1" id="pv1a"><label class="form-check-label" for="pv1a">2</label></div>
    <div class="form-check"><input class="form-check-input" type="radio" name="pv1" id="pv1b"><label class="form-check-label" for="pv1b">4</label></div>
    <div class="form-check mb-2"><input class="form-check-input" type="radio" name="pv1" id="pv1c"><label class="form-check-label" for="pv1c">9</label></div>
    <div id="pv1-feedback" class="mt-1 fw-bold"></div>
  </div>
  <div class="mb-4">
    <p><strong>Question 2:</strong> Python uses 0-based indices. How do you access the <strong>third</strong> element of <code>y = [9, 3, 7, 2]</code>?</p>
    <div class="form-check"><input class="form-check-input" type="radio" name="pv2" id="pv2a"><label class="form-check-label" for="pv2a"><code>y[3]</code></label></div>
    <div class="form-check"><input class="form-check-input" type="radio" name="pv2" id="pv2b"><label class="form-check-label" for="pv2b"><code>y[2]</code></label></div>
    <div class="form-check mb-2"><input class="form-check-input" type="radio" name="pv2" id="pv2c"><label class="form-check-label" for="pv2c"><code>y[1]</code></label></div>
    <div id="pv2-feedback" class="mt-1 fw-bold"></div>
  </div>
  <div class="mb-4">
    <p><strong>Question 3:</strong> Why does <code>sum(np.array([True, False, True, True]))</code> return <code>3</code>?</p>
    <div class="form-check"><input class="form-check-input" type="radio" name="pv3" id="pv3a"><label class="form-check-label" for="pv3a">Because the array has 3 elements</label></div>
    <div class="form-check"><input class="form-check-input" type="radio" name="pv3" id="pv3b"><label class="form-check-label" for="pv3b">Because Python treats True as 1 and False as 0 – sum() therefore counts the True values</label></div>
    <div class="form-check mb-2"><input class="form-check-input" type="radio" name="pv3" id="pv3c"><label class="form-check-label" for="pv3c">Because sum() only counts the first three elements</label></div>
    <div id="pv3-feedback" class="mt-1 fw-bold"></div>
  </div>
  <div class="mb-4">
    <p><strong>Question 4:</strong> What does <code>y[y &lt; 5]</code> return when <code>y = np.array([9, 3, 7, 2])</code>?</p>
    <div class="form-check"><input class="form-check-input" type="radio" name="pv4" id="pv4a"><label class="form-check-label" for="pv4a">[9, 7]</label></div>
    <div class="form-check"><input class="form-check-input" type="radio" name="pv4" id="pv4b"><label class="form-check-label" for="pv4b">[3, 2]</label></div>
    <div class="form-check mb-2"><input class="form-check-input" type="radio" name="pv4" id="pv4c"><label class="form-check-label" for="pv4c">[True, False, True, True]</label></div>
    <div id="pv4-feedback" class="mt-1 fw-bold"></div>
  </div>
  <button onclick="checkPyVektorQuiz()" class="btn btn-primary w-100" style="background-color:#1a4d1a;border:none;font-weight:700;">CHECK ANSWERS</button>
  <div id="py-vektor-result" class="mt-3 fw-bold text-center"></div>
</div>
<script>
function checkPyVektorQuiz() {
  var score=0,total=4;
  var pv1=document.querySelector('input[name="pv1"]:checked'),pv1f=document.getElementById('pv1-feedback');
  if(pv1&&pv1.id==='pv1b'){pv1f.innerHTML='✅ Correct! The list has 4 elements: 9, 3, 7, 2.';pv1f.style.color='#1a4d1a';score++;}
  else if(pv1){pv1f.innerHTML='❌ Not quite. Count the elements: 9, 3, 7, 2 – that is 4.';pv1f.style.color='#d9534f';}
  var pv2=document.querySelector('input[name="pv2"]:checked'),pv2f=document.getElementById('pv2-feedback');
  if(pv2&&pv2.id==='pv2b'){pv2f.innerHTML='✅ Exactly! In Python counting starts at 0. The third element therefore has index 2: y[0]=9, y[1]=3, y[2]=7.';pv2f.style.color='#1a4d1a';score++;}
  else if(pv2){pv2f.innerHTML='❌ Not correct. Python is 0-based: y[0]=9 (1st), y[1]=3 (2nd), y[2]=7 (3rd).';pv2f.style.color='#d9534f';}
  var pv3=document.querySelector('input[name="pv3"]:checked'),pv3f=document.getElementById('pv3-feedback');
  if(pv3&&pv3.id==='pv3b'){pv3f.innerHTML='✅ Exactly! In Python too, True = 1 and False = 0. sum([1,0,1,1]) = 3.';pv3f.style.color='#1a4d1a';score++;}
  else if(pv3){pv3f.innerHTML='❌ Not right. True = 1 and False = 0 – sum() counts the True values.';pv3f.style.color='#d9534f';}
  var pv4=document.querySelector('input[name="pv4"]:checked'),pv4f=document.getElementById('pv4-feedback');
  if(pv4&&pv4.id==='pv4b'){pv4f.innerHTML='✅ Correct! y < 5 tests each element: 9→F, 3→T, 7→F, 2→T. So 3 and 2 are returned.';pv4f.style.color='#1a4d1a';score++;}
  else if(pv4){pv4f.innerHTML='❌ Not quite. Try it in the sandbox! y[y < 5] only returns elements where the condition is True.';pv4f.style.color='#d9534f';}
  var res=document.getElementById('py-vektor-result');
  if(score===total){res.innerHTML='🎉 All questions correct!';res.style.color='#1a4d1a';markQuizDone('w1_q_vectors');}
  else{res.innerHTML=score+' of '+total+' correct. Review the explanations!';res.style.color='#d9534f';}
}
</script>
```

---

### **Exercise 2:** Matrices

```{=html}
<div class="quiz-card">
  <h5>QUIZ: MATRICES</h5>
  <p class="text-muted">🧪 Try out your code here:</p>
```

```{shinylive-python}
#| standalone: true
#| viewerHeight: 230
#| packages: [numpy]
from shiny import App, ui, render, reactive
import io, contextlib, ast
import numpy as np

app_ui = ui.page_fluid(
    ui.input_text_area("code", "Sandbox – Matrices", value="import numpy as np\nX = np.arange(1, 7).reshape(2, 3)\nprint(X)\nprint(X.shape)", width='100%', height='100px'),
    ui.input_action_button("run", "Run Code", class_="btn-success"),
    ui.output_text_verbatim("out")
)
def server(input, output, session):
    @render.text
    @reactive.event(input.run)
    async def out():
        f = io.StringIO(); ns = {}
        with contextlib.redirect_stdout(f):
            try:
                tree = ast.parse(input.code())
                for node in tree.body:
                    if isinstance(node, ast.Expr):
                        val = eval(compile(ast.Expression(node.value),'<s>','eval'), ns)
                        if val is not None: print(val)
                    else: exec(compile(ast.Module([node],[]),'<s>','exec'), ns)
            except Exception as e: print(f"Error: {e}")
        return f.getvalue()
app = App(app_ui, server)
```

```{=html}
  <div class="mb-4 mt-3">
    <p><strong>Question 1:</strong> What does <code>X.shape</code> return for <code>np.arange(1,7).reshape(2,3)</code>?</p>
    <div class="form-check"><input class="form-check-input" type="radio" name="pm1" id="pm1a"><label class="form-check-label" for="pm1a">6</label></div>
    <div class="form-check"><input class="form-check-input" type="radio" name="pm1" id="pm1b"><label class="form-check-label" for="pm1b">(2, 3)</label></div>
    <div class="form-check mb-2"><input class="form-check-input" type="radio" name="pm1" id="pm1c"><label class="form-check-label" for="pm1c">(3, 2)</label></div>
    <div id="pm1-feedback" class="mt-1 fw-bold"></div>
  </div>
  <div class="mb-4">
    <p><strong>Question 2:</strong> What is the difference between <code>np.column_stack()</code> and <code>np.row_stack()</code>?</p>
    <div class="form-check"><input class="form-check-input" type="radio" name="pm2" id="pm2a"><label class="form-check-label" for="pm2a">No difference</label></div>
    <div class="form-check"><input class="form-check-input" type="radio" name="pm2" id="pm2b"><label class="form-check-label" for="pm2b"><code>column_stack()</code> joins vectors as columns, <code>row_stack()</code> as rows</label></div>
    <div class="form-check mb-2"><input class="form-check-input" type="radio" name="pm2" id="pm2c"><label class="form-check-label" for="pm2c"><code>column_stack()</code> only works for numbers</label></div>
    <div id="pm2-feedback" class="mt-1 fw-bold"></div>
  </div>
  <div class="mb-4">
    <p><strong>Question 3:</strong> What does <code>X[1, :]</code> return?</p>
    <div class="form-check"><input class="form-check-input" type="radio" name="pm3" id="pm3a"><label class="form-check-label" for="pm3a">The element at row 1, column 0</label></div>
    <div class="form-check"><input class="form-check-input" type="radio" name="pm3" id="pm3b"><label class="form-check-label" for="pm3b">All elements of the second row (index 1)</label></div>
    <div class="form-check mb-2"><input class="form-check-input" type="radio" name="pm3" id="pm3c"><label class="form-check-label" for="pm3c">All elements of the second column</label></div>
    <div id="pm3-feedback" class="mt-1 fw-bold"></div>
  </div>
  <button onclick="checkPyMatrixQuiz()" class="btn btn-primary w-100" style="background-color:#1a4d1a;border:none;font-weight:700;">CHECK ANSWERS</button>
  <div id="py-matrix-result" class="mt-3 fw-bold text-center"></div>
</div>
<script>
function checkPyMatrixQuiz() {
  var score=0,total=3;
  var pm1=document.querySelector('input[name="pm1"]:checked'),pm1f=document.getElementById('pm1-feedback');
  if(pm1&&pm1.id==='pm1b'){pm1f.innerHTML='✅ Correct! .shape returns (rows, columns) – so (2, 3).';pm1f.style.color='#1a4d1a';score++;}
  else if(pm1){pm1f.innerHTML='❌ Not quite. .shape always returns (rows, columns).';pm1f.style.color='#d9534f';}
  var pm2=document.querySelector('input[name="pm2"]:checked'),pm2f=document.getElementById('pm2-feedback');
  if(pm2&&pm2.id==='pm2b'){pm2f.innerHTML='✅ Exactly! column_stack = join as columns, row_stack = join as rows.';pm2f.style.color='#1a4d1a';score++;}
  else if(pm2){pm2f.innerHTML='❌ Not right. column_stack = columns, row_stack = rows.';pm2f.style.color='#d9534f';}
  var pm3=document.querySelector('input[name="pm3"]:checked'),pm3f=document.getElementById('pm3-feedback');
  if(pm3&&pm3.id==='pm3b'){pm3f.innerHTML='✅ Correct! X[1, :] means: row with index 1 (= 2nd row), all columns (:).';pm3f.style.color='#1a4d1a';score++;}
  else if(pm3){pm3f.innerHTML='❌ Not quite. X[1, :] = row 1, all columns (: = everything).';pm3f.style.color='#d9534f';}
  var res=document.getElementById('py-matrix-result');
  if(score===total){res.innerHTML='🎉 Perfect!';res.style.color='#1a4d1a';markQuizDone('w1_q_matrices');}
  else{res.innerHTML=score+' of '+total+' correct.';res.style.color='#d9534f';}
}
</script>
```

---

### **Exercise 3:** Dictionaries

```{=html}
<div class="quiz-card">
  <h5>QUIZ: DICTIONARIES</h5>
  <p class="text-muted">🧪 Try out your code here:</p>
```

```{shinylive-python}
#| standalone: true
#| viewerHeight: 220
from shiny import App, ui, render, reactive
import io, contextlib, ast
app_ui = ui.page_fluid(
    ui.input_text_area("code", "Sandbox – Dictionaries", value="dict1 = {'color': ['blue', 'red'], 'size': 5, 'state': True}\nprint(type(dict1['color']))\nprint(dict1['color'][1])", width='100%', height='100px'),
    ui.input_action_button("run", "Run Code", class_="btn-success"),
    ui.output_text_verbatim("out")
)
def server(input, output, session):
    @render.text
    @reactive.event(input.run)
    async def out():
        f = io.StringIO(); ns = {}
        with contextlib.redirect_stdout(f):
            try:
                tree = ast.parse(input.code())
                for node in tree.body:
                    if isinstance(node, ast.Expr):
                        val = eval(compile(ast.Expression(node.value),'<s>','eval'), ns)
                        if val is not None: print(val)
                    else: exec(compile(ast.Module([node],[]),'<s>','exec'), ns)
            except Exception as e: print(f"Error: {e}")
        return f.getvalue()
app = App(app_ui, server)
```

```{=html}
  <div class="mb-4 mt-3">
    <p><strong>Question 1:</strong> How do you access the value of key <code>'color'</code> in <code>dict1 = {'color': ['blue', 'red'], 'size': 5}</code>?</p>
    <div class="form-check"><input class="form-check-input" type="radio" name="pl1" id="pl1a"><label class="form-check-label" for="pl1a"><code>dict1[0]</code></label></div>
    <div class="form-check"><input class="form-check-input" type="radio" name="pl1" id="pl1b"><label class="form-check-label" for="pl1b"><code>dict1['color']</code></label></div>
    <div class="form-check mb-2"><input class="form-check-input" type="radio" name="pl1" id="pl1c"><label class="form-check-label" for="pl1c"><code>dict1.color</code></label></div>
    <div id="pl1-feedback" class="mt-1 fw-bold"></div>
  </div>
  <div class="mb-4">
    <p><strong>Question 2:</strong> How do you access the <strong>second position</strong> of the value of <code>'color'</code>?</p>
    <div class="form-check"><input class="form-check-input" type="radio" name="pl2" id="pl2a"><label class="form-check-label" for="pl2a"><code>dict1['color'][2]</code></label></div>
    <div class="form-check"><input class="form-check-input" type="radio" name="pl2" id="pl2b"><label class="form-check-label" for="pl2b"><code>dict1['color'][1]</code></label></div>
    <div class="form-check mb-2"><input class="form-check-input" type="radio" name="pl2" id="pl2c"><label class="form-check-label" for="pl2c"><code>dict1[1]['color']</code></label></div>
    <div id="pl2-feedback" class="mt-1 fw-bold"></div>
  </div>
  <div class="mb-4">
    <p><strong>Question 3:</strong> How do you delete key <code>'size'</code> from a dictionary?</p>
    <div class="form-check"><input class="form-check-input" type="radio" name="pl3" id="pl3a"><label class="form-check-label" for="pl3a"><code>dict1['size'] = None</code></label></div>
    <div class="form-check"><input class="form-check-input" type="radio" name="pl3" id="pl3b"><label class="form-check-label" for="pl3b"><code>del dict1['size']</code></label></div>
    <div class="form-check mb-2"><input class="form-check-input" type="radio" name="pl3" id="pl3c"><label class="form-check-label" for="pl3c"><code>dict1.remove('size')</code></label></div>
    <div id="pl3-feedback" class="mt-1 fw-bold"></div>
  </div>
  <button onclick="checkPyDictQuiz()" class="btn btn-primary w-100" style="background-color:#1a4d1a;border:none;font-weight:700;">CHECK ANSWERS</button>
  <div id="py-dict-result" class="mt-3 fw-bold text-center"></div>
</div>
<script>
function checkPyDictQuiz() {
  var score=0,total=3;
  var pl1=document.querySelector('input[name="pl1"]:checked'),pl1f=document.getElementById('pl1-feedback');
  if(pl1&&pl1.id==='pl1b'){pl1f.innerHTML='✅ Correct! Dictionaries are accessed via their key name.';pl1f.style.color='#1a4d1a';score++;}
  else if(pl1){pl1f.innerHTML='❌ Not quite. Dictionaries are accessed by key, not by index.';pl1f.style.color='#d9534f';}
  var pl2=document.querySelector('input[name="pl2"]:checked'),pl2f=document.getElementById('pl2-feedback');
  if(pl2&&pl2.id==='pl2b'){pl2f.innerHTML='✅ Correct! dict1["color"] returns ["blue", "red"]. The second element has index 1 (0-based): dict1["color"][1] = "red".';pl2f.style.color='#1a4d1a';score++;}
  else if(pl2){pl2f.innerHTML='❌ Not correct. First get the value: dict1["color"] → ["blue", "red"]. Then: [1] (0-based).';pl2f.style.color='#d9534f';}
  var pl3=document.querySelector('input[name="pl3"]:checked'),pl3f=document.getElementById('pl3-feedback');
  if(pl3&&pl3.id==='pl3b'){pl3f.innerHTML='✅ Exactly! del dict1["size"] removes the key along with its value completely.';pl3f.style.color='#1a4d1a';score++;}
  else if(pl3){pl3f.innerHTML='❌ Not right. = None leaves the key in place. del removes it completely.';pl3f.style.color='#d9534f';}
  var res=document.getElementById('py-dict-result');
  if(score===total){res.innerHTML='🎉 All correct!';res.style.color='#1a4d1a';markQuizDone('w1_q_lists');}
  else{res.innerHTML=score+' of '+total+' correct.';res.style.color='#d9534f';}
}
</script>
```

---

### **Exercise 4:** DataFrames

```{=html}
<div class="quiz-card">
  <h5>QUIZ: DATAFRAMES</h5>
  <p class="text-muted">🧪 Try out your code here:</p>
```

```{shinylive-python}
#| standalone: true
#| viewerHeight: 250
from shiny import App, ui, render, reactive
import io, contextlib, ast
app_ui = ui.page_fluid(
    ui.input_text_area("code", "Sandbox – DataFrames", value="import pandas as pd\nProduct_name = ['orange', 'strawberry', 'broccoli', 'blueberry', 'cucumber']\nFruit = [True, True, False, True, False]\nColor = ['orange', 'red', 'green', 'blue', 'green']\ndf = pd.DataFrame({'Product_name': Product_name, 'Fruit': Fruit, 'Color': Color})\nprint(df)", width='100%', height='140px'),
    ui.input_action_button("run", "Run Code", class_="btn-success"),
    ui.output_text_verbatim("out")
)
def server(input, output, session):
    @render.text
    @reactive.event(input.run)
    async def out():
        f = io.StringIO(); ns = {}
        with contextlib.redirect_stdout(f):
            try:
                tree = ast.parse(input.code())
                for node in tree.body:
                    if isinstance(node, ast.Expr):
                        val = eval(compile(ast.Expression(node.value),'<s>','eval'), ns)
                        if val is not None: print(val)
                    else: exec(compile(ast.Module([node],[]),'<s>','exec'), ns)
            except Exception as e: print(f"Error: {e}")
        return f.getvalue()
app = App(app_ui, server)
```

```{=html}
  <div class="mb-4 mt-3">
    <p><strong>Question 1:</strong> What is the most important difference between a pandas DataFrame and a NumPy matrix?</p>
    <div class="form-check"><input class="form-check-input" type="radio" name="pd1" id="pd1a"><label class="form-check-label" for="pd1a">DataFrames can be larger</label></div>
    <div class="form-check"><input class="form-check-input" type="radio" name="pd1" id="pd1b"><label class="form-check-label" for="pd1b">DataFrames can contain different data types in different columns, NumPy arrays only one type</label></div>
    <div class="form-check mb-2"><input class="form-check-input" type="radio" name="pd1" id="pd1c"><label class="form-check-label" for="pd1c">NumPy arrays always have column names</label></div>
    <div id="pd1-feedback" class="mt-1 fw-bold"></div>
  </div>
  <div class="mb-4">
    <p><strong>Question 2:</strong> How many fruits does <code>Fruit = [True, True, False, True, False]</code> contain? Which function do you use?</p>
    <div class="form-check"><input class="form-check-input" type="radio" name="pd2" id="pd2a"><label class="form-check-label" for="pd2a">2, with <code>len(Fruit)</code></label></div>
    <div class="form-check"><input class="form-check-input" type="radio" name="pd2" id="pd2b"><label class="form-check-label" for="pd2b">3, with <code>sum(Fruit)</code></label></div>
    <div class="form-check mb-2"><input class="form-check-input" type="radio" name="pd2" id="pd2c"><label class="form-check-label" for="pd2c">5, with <code>sum(Fruit)</code></label></div>
    <div id="pd2-feedback" class="mt-1 fw-bold"></div>
  </div>
  <div class="mb-4">
    <p><strong>Question 3:</strong> What does <code>pd.merge(Students, Full_Names)</code> do by default?</p>
    <div class="form-check"><input class="form-check-input" type="radio" name="pd3" id="pd3a"><label class="form-check-label" for="pd3a">All rows of both DataFrames are merged, missing values filled with NaN</label></div>
    <div class="form-check"><input class="form-check-input" type="radio" name="pd3" id="pd3b"><label class="form-check-label" for="pd3b">Only rows that appear in both DataFrames are merged (Inner Join)</label></div>
    <div class="form-check mb-2"><input class="form-check-input" type="radio" name="pd3" id="pd3c"><label class="form-check-label" for="pd3c">The DataFrames are stacked on top of each other (rows)</label></div>
    <div id="pd3-feedback" class="mt-1 fw-bold"></div>
  </div>
  <button onclick="checkPyDfQuiz()" class="btn btn-primary w-100" style="background-color:#1a4d1a;border:none;font-weight:700;">CHECK ANSWERS</button>
  <div id="py-df-result" class="mt-3 fw-bold text-center"></div>
</div>
<script>
function checkPyDfQuiz() {
  var score=0,total=3;
  var pd1=document.querySelector('input[name="pd1"]:checked'),pd1f=document.getElementById('pd1-feedback');
  if(pd1&&pd1.id==='pd1b'){pd1f.innerHTML='✅ Correct! A DataFrame can have a column with numbers and one with text – a NumPy array would convert everything to the same type.';pd1f.style.color='#1a4d1a';score++;}
  else if(pd1){pd1f.innerHTML='❌ Not quite. The key difference lies in data types: NumPy arrays contain only one type, DataFrames can mix different types column by column.';pd1f.style.color='#d9534f';}
  var pd2=document.querySelector('input[name="pd2"]:checked'),pd2f=document.getElementById('pd2-feedback');
  if(pd2&&pd2.id==='pd2b'){pd2f.innerHTML='✅ Correct! sum() on a boolean list counts the True values (True=1). There are 3 fruits: orange, strawberry and blueberry.';pd2f.style.color='#1a4d1a';score++;}
  else if(pd2){pd2f.innerHTML='❌ Not right. len()=5 (total). sum() counts True values – the fruits.';pd2f.style.color='#d9534f';}
  var pd3=document.querySelector('input[name="pd3"]:checked'),pd3f=document.getElementById('pd3-feedback');
  if(pd3&&pd3.id==='pd3b'){pd3f.innerHTML='✅ Exactly! Default how="inner" – only rows with matching keys in both DataFrames. Use how="outer" for all rows.';pd3f.style.color='#1a4d1a';score++;}
  else if(pd3){pd3f.innerHTML='❌ Not correct. pd.merge() default = Inner Join. Without how="outer" only matching rows are kept.';pd3f.style.color='#d9534f';}
  var res=document.getElementById('py-df-result');
  if(score===total){res.innerHTML='🎉 Excellent – Week 1 Python completed!';res.style.color='#1a4d1a';markQuizDone('w1_q_dataframes');}
  else{res.innerHTML=score+' of '+total+' correct. Review the explanations and try again!';res.style.color='#d9534f';}
}
</script>
```

## EXERCISES – Data Manipulation

### **Exercise 1:** numpy axis operations & np.where()

```{=html}
<div class="quiz-card">
  <h5>EXERCISE 1: NUMPY AXIS & NP.WHERE()</h5>
  <p class="text-muted">🧪 Solve the tasks in the sandbox:</p>
```

```{shinylive-python}
#| standalone: true
#| viewerHeight: 340
#| packages: [numpy]
from shiny import App, ui, render, reactive
import io, contextlib, ast
import numpy as np

app_ui = ui.page_fluid(
    ui.input_text_area("code", "Exercise 1", value="import numpy as np\nM = np.array([-2,3,1,-5,4,2,0,-1,3,6,-3,2,1,4,-2,5,2,-4,3,1], dtype=float).reshape(5,4)\n\n# Task 1: Calculate the mean of each ROW (axis=1)\n\n# Task 2: Calculate the mean of each COLUMN (axis=0)\n\n# Task 3: Find positions of positive elements (>0) using np.where()\n\n# Task 4: Get the VALUES of positive elements\n", width='100%', height='210px'),
    ui.input_action_button("run", "Run Code", class_="btn-success"),
    ui.output_text_verbatim("out")
)
def server(input, output, session):
    @render.text
    @reactive.event(input.run)
    async def out():
        f = io.StringIO(); ns = {}
        with contextlib.redirect_stdout(f):
            try:
                tree = ast.parse(input.code())
                for node in tree.body:
                    if isinstance(node, ast.Expr):
                        val = eval(compile(ast.Expression(node.value),'<s>','eval'), ns)
                        if val is not None: print(val)
                    else: exec(compile(ast.Module([node],[]),'<s>','exec'), ns)
            except Exception as e: print(f"Error: {e}")
        return f.getvalue()
app = App(app_ui, server)
```

```{=html}
  <button onclick="toggleSolution('py_sol1')" class="btn btn-outline-success mt-2">Show / Hide Solution</button>
  <div id="py_sol1" style="display:none;background:#f8f9fa;border-radius:8px;padding:14px;margin-top:10px;">
    <pre><code># Task 1 – mean of each row:
print(np.mean(M, axis=1))

# Task 2 – mean of each column:
print(np.mean(M, axis=0))

# Task 3 – positions of positive elements:
print(np.where(M > 0))

# Task 4 – values of positive elements:
print(M[np.where(M > 0)])</code></pre>
  </div>
</div>
```

---

### **Exercise 2:** if / elif / else

```{=html}
<div class="quiz-card">
  <h5>EXERCISE 2: IF STATEMENTS</h5>
  <p class="text-muted">🧪 Complete and extend the if statement:</p>
```

```{shinylive-python}
#| standalone: true
#| viewerHeight: 300
from shiny import App, ui, render, reactive
import io, contextlib, ast

app_ui = ui.page_fluid(
    ui.input_text_area("code", "Exercise 2", value="a = 3\n\n# Task 1: Write an if/else so that:\n# - 'a is positive' is printed if a > 0\n# - 'a is negative' is printed otherwise\n\n# Task 2: Add an elif to also print 'a is zero' if a == 0\n# Try a = 0, a = 5, a = -3\n\n", width='100%', height='170px'),
    ui.input_action_button("run", "Run Code", class_="btn-success"),
    ui.output_text_verbatim("out")
)
def server(input, output, session):
    @render.text
    @reactive.event(input.run)
    async def out():
        f = io.StringIO(); ns = {}
        with contextlib.redirect_stdout(f):
            try:
                tree = ast.parse(input.code())
                for node in tree.body:
                    if isinstance(node, ast.Expr):
                        val = eval(compile(ast.Expression(node.value),'<s>','eval'), ns)
                        if val is not None: print(val)
                    else: exec(compile(ast.Module([node],[]),'<s>','exec'), ns)
            except Exception as e: print(f"Error: {e}")
        return f.getvalue()
app = App(app_ui, server)
```

```{=html}
  <button onclick="toggleSolution('py_sol2')" class="btn btn-outline-success mt-2">Show / Hide Solution</button>
  <div id="py_sol2" style="display:none;background:#f8f9fa;border-radius:8px;padding:14px;margin-top:10px;">
    <pre><code>a = 3

if a > 0:
    print("a is positive")
elif a == 0:
    print("a is zero")
else:
    print("a is negative")</code></pre>
  </div>
</div>
```

---

### **Exercise 3 *(Expert)*:** map() + ternary expression

```{=html}
<div class="quiz-card">
  <h5>EXERCISE 3 ⭐ EXPERT: MAP() + TERNARY</h5>
  <p class="text-muted">🧪 Combine map() and a ternary expression:</p>
```

```{shinylive-python}
#| standalone: true
#| viewerHeight: 280
from shiny import App, ui, render, reactive
import io, contextlib, ast

app_ui = ui.page_fluid(
    ui.input_text_area("code", "Exercise 3", value="import math\ny = [1, 3, -5, 4, -10]\n\n# Task: Use map() with a lambda to:\n# - return sqrt(element) if element > 0\n# - return 0 otherwise\n# Hint: list(map(lambda x: ... , y))\n", width='100%', height='160px'),
    ui.input_action_button("run", "Run Code", class_="btn-success"),
    ui.output_text_verbatim("out")
)
def server(input, output, session):
    @render.text
    @reactive.event(input.run)
    async def out():
        f = io.StringIO(); ns = {}
        with contextlib.redirect_stdout(f):
            try:
                tree = ast.parse(input.code())
                for node in tree.body:
                    if isinstance(node, ast.Expr):
                        val = eval(compile(ast.Expression(node.value),'<s>','eval'), ns)
                        if val is not None: print(val)
                    else: exec(compile(ast.Module([node],[]),'<s>','exec'), ns)
            except Exception as e: print(f"Error: {e}")
        return f.getvalue()
app = App(app_ui, server)
```

```{=html}
  <button onclick="toggleSolution('py_sol3')" class="btn btn-outline-success mt-2">Show / Hide Solution</button>
  <div id="py_sol3" style="display:none;background:#f8f9fa;border-radius:8px;padding:14px;margin-top:10px;">
    <pre><code>import math
y = [1, 3, -5, 4, -10]

result = list(map(lambda x: math.sqrt(x) if x > 0 else 0, y))
print(result)</code></pre>
  </div>
</div>
```
:::
---

## Offline Materials

### Lecture Material

These are the slides used in the lecture of Week 1 and the videos of the matching lecture.

```{=html}
<div class="d-flex gap-3 mb-3 flex-wrap">
  <a href="WEEK0/week0_lecture_slides.pdf" download class="btn btn-outline-success">
     Week 1 – Basic datatypes &amp; data manipulation (PDF)
  </a>
</div>
```

* **Videos:**
  Video 1/3: [General introduction to RStudio](https://youtu.be/doQYGprZe94)
  Video 2/3: [Working with R scripts](https://youtu.be/joM5D3AXXgg)
  Video 3/3: [Working with Markdown](https://youtu.be/DVOlDfgY9gU)

### Markdown Files (R only)

These files contain the same content as this page and can be opened and executed directly in RStudio:

```{=html}
<div class="d-flex gap-3 mb-3 flex-wrap">
  <a href="WEEK0/00_basic_data_structure.md" download class="btn btn-outline-success">
     Week 1 – Basic Data Structures (Markdown)
  </a>
  <a href="WEEK0/00_basic_data_manipulation.md" download class="btn btn-outline-success">
     Week 1 – Advanced Data Manipulation (Markdown)
  </a>
</div>
```

### Cheat Sheets (R only)

Quick reference cards for the most important R functions:

```{=html}
<div class="d-flex gap-3 mb-4 flex-wrap">
  <a href="WEEK0/base-r.pdf" target="_blank" class="btn btn-outline-danger">R Base – Cheat Sheet (PDF)</a>
  <a href="WEEK0/rmarkdown-reference.pdf" target="_blank" class="btn btn-outline-danger">Markdown Reference (PDF)</a>
  <a href="WEEK0/cheatsheet_Best_Practice_R.pdf" target="_blank" class="btn btn-outline-danger">R Best Practice (PDF)</a>
</div>
```
